name: api-organization
description: Explains the standardized API organization pattern for this codebase. Use when creating new API endpoints, API clients, or modifying existing API structure. Covers the 5-file system (endpoint-types, endpoints, api-client, admin-api-client, protected-endpoints), role-based access patterns (admin vs regular users), and TypeScript type safety across the API layer. All API code lives in src/lib/api/ following this exact pattern.
capabilities: Defines 5-file API structure: endpoint-types, endpoints, api-client, admin-api-client, protected-endpoints
Documents Supabase Auth integration for automatic session handling and token extraction
Explains role-based access patterns for admin vs regular user endpoints
Provides TypeScript type safety from params through responses
Shows environment variable configuration for API base URLs
author: BOM-98
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown files with architectural guidance. All static findings are false positives triggered by code examples in documentation blocks. No executable code, network calls, file system access beyond its own files, or code execution capabilities exist. The backticks, environment variable references, and URLs detected are documentation examples, not actual shell commands, credential access, or network requests.
valueStatement: API code becomes scattered and inconsistent across a codebase. This skill provides a standardized 5-file pattern for organizing API endpoints, types, and clients with full TypeScript safety and role-based access control.
limitations: Does not include executable code templates - only documentation patterns
Requires manual implementation of the 5-file structure

useCases.title: Set up API architecture
useCases.description: Learn the 5-file pattern for organizing API code in Next.js projects with Supabase auth.

useCases.title: Enforce API standards
useCases.description: Establish consistent API structure across team with type-safe patterns and role-based access.

useCases.title: Document auth flows
useCases.description: Understand how Supabase auth integrates with API clients for automatic authentication.

promptTemplates.title: Add new endpoint
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to add types in endpoint-types.ts for a new 'transactions' domain with CRUD operations. Include params, responses, and body types.

promptTemplates.title: Define endpoint URLs
promptTemplates.prompt: How should I structure endpoints.ts for a 'budgets' resource? Show the pattern for list, get, create, update, and delete operations.

promptTemplates.title: Create wrapper functions
promptTemplates.prompt: Create wrapper functions in protected-endpoints.ts for a 'categories' domain. Include automatic auth via Supabase.

promptTemplates.title: Add admin access
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I add an admin-only endpoint for the 'users' domain? Show the admin-api-client pattern with role checking.

bestPractices: Define all TypeScript types before implementing endpoint functions for type safety
Use getAuthHeaders() in api-client to extract Supabase access token automatically
Call checkAdminPermission() before any admin-only API operations
Keep endpoint URLs in one centralized file for easy maintenance
antiPatterns: Hardcoding URLs instead of using API_ENDPOINTS constants
Skipping type definitions and using 'any' for API responses
Making API calls directly without using the protected-endpoints wrapper
Forgetting to call checkAdminPermission() before admin operations

faq.question: Does this skill work with other auth providers?
faq.answer: Yes. The 5-file pattern is auth-agnostic. Replace getAuthHeaders() to use your auth provider's token extraction.

faq.question: What is the maximum number of endpoints supported?
faq.answer: No hard limit. Each domain in endpoint-types.ts maps to endpoints.ts. Add as many domains as needed.

faq.question: How do I integrate with existing API code?
faq.answer: Migrate URLs to endpoints.ts, define types in endpoint-types.ts, replace fetch calls with apiGet/apiPost wrappers.

faq.question: Is my API data stored securely?
faq.answer: The pattern uses Supabase httpOnly cookies and RLS policies. Security depends on proper Supabase configuration.

faq.question: Why is my admin check failing?
faq.answer: Verify the users table has correct role values. Call checkAdminPermission() before admin operations. Check user session exists.

faq.question: How is this different from standard fetch?
faq.answer: This pattern adds type safety, centralized auth, role-based access, and consistent error handling across all API calls.
